all_recent.body:
*** The Huntress Agent has been tasked to isolate this host from the rest of the network in order to prevent the incident from spreading to other hosts. ***

If you have an urgent request for support, please go to the link below to place a request a callback from SOC Support.
https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429258/infection_reports/1866108/soc_callback_requests/new

Host: WKISI6 - https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429258/agents/11407683
Organization: ISI - International Sportsmedicine Institute
Tags: None
Security Products: CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor

Incident Report: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429258/infection_reports/1866108
Severity: Critical

Investigative Summary:
----------------------
At 2026-01-02 21:15:33 UTC, a Windows Startup with the name "GawUjxu" was detected on the endpoint WKISI6 under the user Administrator, utilizing regsvr32.exe within the AppData directory. 

The last logged-in user was "sKieffer / S-1-5-21-2085862506-2924907105-3116931485-1112". Crowdstrike detected and quarantined the malicious autorun activity. 

Huntress noted during the investigation that another startup service, "GotoMeeting," was created at the same time as  "GawUjxu". Attackers use regsvr32.exe to run malicious code hosted on remote servers. By using a trusted Microsoft process to execute their code, they can sometimes bypass application whitelisting or firewalls.

Huntress has isolated the host to prevent further malicious activity. Huntress also recommends reimaging the host to a known clean operating system to ensure no further persistence remains. See the recommendations below.

Details:

Autorun Name: GawUjxu
Path: regsvr32.exe "C:\Users\administrator\AppData\Roaming\Kafew\Qogavj.dll"
SHA256: 022cb167a29a32dae848be91aef721c74f1975af151807dafcc5ed832db246b7 

Autorun Name: GoToMeeting
Binary Path: "C:\Program Files (x86)\GoToMeeting\19950\g2mstart.exe" "/Trigger RunAtLogon"
SHA256: 1ddb4e75b45852989ba34368a90d12463f85b378cbd860dfe543faad10e17d5f  

Threat Descriptions:
--------------------
Suspicious RMM: A suspicious remote monitoring and management (RMM) application was detected on this endpoint. These applications may be benign within the environment, or levied by adversaries as a method of persistence. Occasionally software vendors will additionally distribute these RMM's as a portion of their support process. 

Remediations:
-------------
Assisted Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC to remediate the incident. These can be executed automatically in the Huntress Platform:
- Delete Registry Value - key: HKU\S-1-5-21-2085862506-2924907105-3116931485-500\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run + value: GawUjxu
- Delete Registry Value - key: HKU\S-1-5-21-2085862506-2924907105-3116931485-500\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run + value: GoToMeeting
- Delete Registry Value - key: HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run + value: GoToMeetingInstall19992
- Delete Registry Value - key: HKLM\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run + value: GoToMeetingInstall19950
- Delete File - path: c:\users\administrator\appdata\roaming\kafew\qogavj.dll
- Delete File - path: c:\program files (x86)\gotomeeting\19950\g2mstart.exe + sha256: 1ddb4e75b45852989ba34368a90d12463f85b378cbd860dfe543faad10e17d5f
- Delete File - path: c:\program files (x86)\gotomeeting\19992\g2minstaller.exe + sha256: 415e68bbf6bf7b5ca6090c74b924860b66a6d12e322d142257ef8e9e5e8f2edb
- Delete File - path: c:\program files (x86)\gotomeeting\19950\g2minstaller.exe + sha256: 1ddb4e75b45852989ba34368a90d12463f85b378cbd860dfe543faad10e17d5f
- Delete File - path: C:\Users\administrator\AppData\Roaming\Kafew\\Comux
- Delete File - path: C:\Users\administrator\AppData\Roaming\Kafew\\GoveLkew
- + 5 more

Manual Remediations provided by the Huntress SOC are highly recommended remediation actions to be conducted by your team before resolving the incident in the Huntress Platform:
- Identify and remove all unauthorized software, scripts, or services from the system.
- Reimage the system if compromise is extensive or trust in integrity cannot be restored.
- Audit affected directories/files for additional suspicious files and remove those found.
- Reset credentials for the affected system's user(s) and any privileged accounts recently used on the system.
- Enroll the user in a security awareness training session to reinforce safe practices in the corporate environment.

All remediations provided can be found in the Huntress Platform: Incident Report: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429258/infection_reports/1866108#remediations-tab

Lead Signal Information:
------------------------
Signal Name: Regsvr32 Dll Within Appdata
Detected At: 2026-01-02 21:15:29 UTC
Registry Key: HKU\S-1-5-21-2085862506-2924907105-3116931485-500\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run
Registry Value: GawUjxu
Registry Data: regsvr32.exe "C:\Users\administrator\AppData\Roaming\Kafew\Qogavj.dll"
File Path: c:\windows\system32\regsvr32.exe

All investigated signals can be found in the Huntress Platform: https://technijian.huntress.io/org/429258/infection_reports/1866108#signals-investigated-tab

-------------------------
Thanks again for trusting Huntress and please don't hesitate to reach out to incidents@huntress.io if you have any questions.

items.action: Identify and remove all unauthorized software, scripts, or services from the system.
Reimage the system if compromise is extensive or trust in integrity cannot be restored.
Audit affected directories/files for additional suspicious files and remove those found.
Reset credentials for the affected system's user(s) and any privileged accounts recently used on the system.
Enroll the user in a security awareness training session to reinforce safe practices in the corporate environment.

all_recent.subject: CRITICAL - ISOLATED - Incident on WKISI6 (ISI - International Sportsmedicine Institute)
